channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Farmers, business owners, fire survivors face uncertainty after $100B in disaster relief flounders
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — American farmers, small business owners and wildfire survivors are among those who will suffer if Congress cannot agree on a new spending bill after President-elect Donald Trump abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan that included more than $100 billion in disaster aid.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, LEAH WILLINGHAM and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder 'acceptable' among 40% of young people
item.description: The poll was conducted the week after a man, suspected to be 26-year-old Luigi Mangione, allegedly shot and killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Bryan Thompson over his discontent with the American healthcare system.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New House GOP budget proposal revives Farm Bill extension and aid money but drops ethanol provision
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — American farmers are hoping that aid to agriculture will be revived as Congress struggles to pass a short-term spending bill that would keep the federal government funded and avert a looming partial government shutdown set to begin after midnight Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: President-elect Trump on Thursday backed a new spending deal brokered by House Republicans to avert a shutdown after an initial proposal was torpedoed amid opposition from Trump and several of his allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veteran Fox News business anchor Neil Cavuto leaving after 28 years
item.description: Neil Cavuto, a business journalist who hosts a weekday afternoon show on Fox News Channel and has been with the network since its inception in 1996, is leaving after Thursday's show, Fox said.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: 76ers win city council nod to build $1.3B downtown stadium and leave South Philadelphia
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The Philadelphia 76ers won city council approval on Thursday for a $1.3 billion downtown arena despite vocal opposition from nearby Chinatown residents and other activists. Council voted 12-5 to approve the project at a packed meeting at City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers strike at facilities around the country as Teamsters seek contract
item.description: Amazon says it doesn’t expect an impact on its operations during what the union calls the largest strike against the company in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, ABC 4

item.title: James Gunn's 'Superman' trailer teases DC characters rarely seen in live-action movies
item.description: The trailer — which debuted one day after another, much shorter teaser — was posted to the film's social media pages and the DC Comics YouTube page at 9 a.m. Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois man fixes bikes for the less fortunate
item.description: "Growing up as a kid, I never had a new bike, but I did have a bike," said Chuck's Bikes and Blessings owner Chuck Henderson.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the most Googled Christmas cookie recipes in every state
item.description: Google Trends released a map of the most-searched-for cookies by state, and the recipe that's most popular in your state might just surprise you.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, Fox 5

item.title: UPDATE: Medical Examiner identifies Mahomet homicide suspect killed in Berwyn
item.description: Update at 2:45 p.m. The Cook County Medical Examiner's Office has released the name of the man suspected of killing three people in Mahomet Wednesday night before fleeing to Berwyn, where he was shot dead by police. The Medical Examiner's Office identified him as 24-year-old John R.
item.dc:creator: Heather Robinson, NewsNation

item.title: Abbott to unveil new border security strategy
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott plans to unveil what his staff is calling a "new border security strategy" at a ranch outside Eagle Pass, Texas, on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Immigration drives US population growth to highest rate in 23 years as residents pass 340 million
item.description: Immigration in 2024 drove U.S. population growth to its fastest rate in 23 years as the nation surpassed 340 million residents.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles deputy mayor allegedly made bomb threat to City Hall
item.description: Federal agents searched the home of Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Brian Williams on Tuesday following allegations that he made a bomb threat to City Hall earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Fox 5

item.title: Congress battles over looming government shutdown and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KRON4

item.title: Man accused of running secret Chinese gov police station in NYC makes plea: US Attorney
item.description: One of two New York City residents accused of operating a secret Chinese police station in Chinatown pleaded guilty in federal court to conspiring to act as an agent of China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security bill clears first hurdle in Senate
item.description: The Senate voted 73-27 to advance the measure, which could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans if passed before Congress leaves this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KRON4

item.title: Florida pursues charge against suspect in Trump assassination attempt for a crash after his arrest
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida authorities announced Wednesday that they're pursuing a criminal case against a man accused of trying to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump for a car crash that occurred after law enforcement stopped traffic to catch him.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 500,000 winter tires recalled due to poor traction in snow
item.description: A tire company that sells winter tires has issued a recall for more than 540,000 of its snow tires, which do not comply with North American standards due to lack of proper traction on snow.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Scientists find 'forever chemicals' lurking in certain smartwatch wristbands
item.description: Prior research has shown that more than a fifth of Americans wear a smartwatch or a fitness tracker — and they do so for extended periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, KRON4

item.title: Electoral College formally votes to confirm Trump's win
item.description: President-elect Trump has clinched the necessary number of Electoral College votes to become the next occupant of the White House, teeing up the official certification of his victory before Congress next month.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: A Republican brouhaha is emerging after President-elect Trump called to eliminate daylight saving time, setting up battle lines between prominent GOP lawmakers in their hopes to lock the clock. Trump waded into the longtime sore subject Friday, surprising — and perplexing — some lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man convicted of quadruple homicide is put to death in Indiana's 1st execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man convicted of killing four people including his brother and his sister’s fiancé decades ago was put to death early Wednesday, marking the state’s first execution in 15 years. Joseph Corcoran, 49, was pronounced dead at 12:44 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon has up to 20% off gift cards ahead of the holidays
item.description: You can save on a wide range of gift cards, making it easier and more affordable to give the gift of shopping and saving.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Community members wrestle with grief in aftermath of Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Community members in Wisconsin continued to wrestle with grief and called for change in the aftermath of a school shooting that killed a teacher and a student and wounded six others.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Maryland shooting and fiery car crash: police
item.description: One person has died and nine others were injured following a shooting and related fiery car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, Fox 5

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We’re still two weeks away from the end of 2024, but maybe you’re already gearing up for tax season (we are more than 100 days out from Tax Day, in case you’re curious).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James returns to score 18 in Lakers' win vs Grizzlies after missing 2 games with foot injury
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James returned to the Los Angeles Lakers on Sunday night in a 116-110 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies after a two-game break. James is fighting a left foot injury and was away from the team most of last week because of personal reasons.
item.dc:creator: KYLE GLASER, Associated Press, Fox 5
